title: pseudognaphalium chartaceum
intro: Pseudognaphalium chartaceum, also known as brittlebush or yellow rabbitbrush, is a shrub-like plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. Its health benefits include treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Pseudognaphalium chartaceum is prepared as an infusion, tea, or tincture in herbal medicine, and its leaves and stems can be dried and used in smudging rituals for their purifying properties.
